:::note
This rule has been implemented but not released yet. It will be available in the next release.
:::
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
## Summary
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/noComponentHookFactories`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**error**](/reference/diagnostics#error).
- This rule belongs to the following domains:
  - [`react`](/linter/domains#react)
- Sources: 
  - Same as [`react-hooks/component-hook-factories`](https://github.com/facebook/react/blob/main/packages/eslint-plugin-react-hooks/README.md)
  - Same as [`react-x/component-hook-factories`](https://eslint-react.xyz/docs/rules/component-hook-factories)
  - Same as [`@eslint-react/component-hook-factories`](https://eslint-react.xyz/docs/rules/component-hook-factories)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noComponentHookFactories": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Disallows defining React components or custom hooks inside other functions.

Defining components or hooks inside other functions creates new instances on every call.
React treats each new instance as a completely different component, which destroys and
recreates the entire component subtree on each render and causes all state to be lost.

## Examples

### Invalid

A component is defined inside a factory function:

```jsx
function makeComponent(label) {
  function MyComponent() {
    return <div>{label}</div>;
  }
  return MyComponent;
}
```

Move it to the module level.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

A hook is defined inside a factory function:

```jsx
function makeHook(key) {
  function useMyHook() {
    return useState(key);
  }
  return useMyHook;
}
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.jsx:2:12 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-component-hook-factories">lint/nursery/noComponentHookFactories</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">✖</span></strong> <span style="color: Tomato;">Do not define hook </span><span style="color: Tomato;"><strong>useMyHook</strong></span><span style="color: Tomato;"> inside a function.</span><br />  <br />    <strong>1 │ </strong>function makeHook(key) &#123;<br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>2 │ </strong>  function useMyHook() &#123;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>           <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>3 │ </strong>    return useState(key);<br />    <strong>4 │ </strong>  &#125;<br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Each call creates a new hook identity, causing React to lose its state across renders. Move it to the module level.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### Valid

Components and hooks defined at the module level:

```jsx
function MyComponent() {
  return <div>Hello</div>;
}

function useMyHook() {
  return useState(0);
}
```

Higher-order components that receive a component as a parameter are allowed:

```jsx
function withAuth(WrappedComponent) {
  function AuthenticatedComponent(props) {
    return <WrappedComponent {...props} />;
  }
  return AuthenticatedComponent;
}
```
